name: From Castrillo de Riopisuerga to Sotresgudo — circular by the Río Pisuegra
name_original: De  Castrillo de Riopisuerga a Sotresgudo — circular por el Río Pisuegra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Herrera de Pisuerga offer diverse terrain, transitioning from the plains of Tierra de Campos to the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. The region is characterized by the confluence of the Pisuerga and Burejo rivers, providing scenic waterside paths. A prominent feature for cyclists is the historic Canal de Castilla, which offers mostly well-paved and traffic-free surfaces. This varied landscape includes river valleys, agricultural areas, and proximity to geological formations like Las Loras Geopark. Best road cycling routes around Herrera De Pisuerga The most popular road cycling route is Castrillo de Villavega from Herrera de Pisuerga – circular via Osorno la Mayor, a 48.7 miles (78.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete, exploring varied regional landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pisuerga River – Hermitage of Santa Cecilia loop from Herrera de Pisuerga, a moderate 40.7 miles (65.5 km) path, following the Pisuerga River and passing local landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the Herrera de Pisuerga – circular by the Canal del Castilla, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through the historic Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 56 minutes. Road cycling around Herrera De Pisuerga is defined by river valleys, the historic Canal de Castilla, and transitional plains, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Herrera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Herrera De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for road cycling, transitioning from the flat plains of Tierra de Campos to the rolling foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. You'll encounter scenic waterside paths along the Pisuerga and Burejo rivers, as well as the historic Canal de Castilla, which provides mostly well-paved and traffic-free surfaces. Routes can vary from gentle agricultural areas to more challenging sections with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Herrera de Pisuerga offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159937897/herrera-de-pisuerga-circular-by-the-canal-del-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de Pisuerga – circular by the Canal del Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is a 14.3-mile (23.0 km) trail with minimal elevation gain, ideal for a relaxed ride. Another easy option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1061616878/from-castrillo-de-riopisuerga-to-sotresgudo-circular-by-the-rio-pisuegra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castrillo de Riopisuerga to Sotresgudo — circular by the Río Pisuegra&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.3 miles (35.9 km) long and follows the river.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on a road cycling tour?
A: Road cycling routes in the area often feature stunning natural beauty. You can enjoy views of the Pisuerga and Burejo rivers, and the historic Canal de Castilla itself is a significant feature. For broader vistas, consider routes that pass near areas like the Las Loras Geopark or the Boedo-Ojeda Comarca, which offer varied and picturesque backdrops. While not directly on all routes, the region's proximity to Peña Amaya and Las Tuerces Natural Monument suggests opportunities for panoramic views.

Q: What kind of landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes incorporate historical and engineering marvels. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Rey Aqueduct (Canal de Castilla)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Andrés Sluice, Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5831684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest. The town of Herrera de Pisuerga itself has Roman archaeological sites and an Interpretation Center of the Canal de Castilla.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Herrera de Pisuerga are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175753241/castrillo-de-villavega-from-herrera-de-pisuerga-circular-via-osorno-la-mayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrillo de Villavega from Herrera de Pisuerga – circular via Osorno la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, and the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159937897/herrera-de-pisuerga-circular-by-the-canal-del-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de Pisuerga – circular by the Canal del Castilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, especially in the plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are cooler, and while cycling is possible, conditions may be less predictable.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied regional landscapes, the well-maintained paths along the Canal de Castilla, and the opportunity to explore both river valleys and agricultural areas. The mix of easy and moderate routes also makes it appealing to a wide range of cyclists.

Q: Are there options for more challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking more challenging rides, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Humada loop from Herrera de Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; offer more substantial elevation gain. This route covers 46.4 miles (74.7 km) and includes over 680 meters of ascent, providing a good workout and often leading to rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: Herrera de Pisuerga, as a starting point for many routes, typically offers various parking options within or near the town center. Look for public parking areas, especially close to the Canal de Castilla or the main urban areas, which often serve as convenient trailheads for cyclists.

Q: Can I access Herrera de Pisuerga by public transport with my bike?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, it's advisable to check local bus or train services for their bike carriage policies. Generally, regional buses might have limited space for bikes, and it's best to inquire directly with the transport provider in advance. Driving to Herrera de Pisuerga is often the most flexible option for cyclists with their own equipment.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs available along the cycling routes or in Herrera de Pisuerga?
A: Herrera de Pisuerga itself offers various amenities, including cafes and pubs where you can refuel before or after your ride. While some longer routes might pass through smaller villages with limited services, the town serves as a good base for refreshments and meals. It's always a good idea to carry snacks and water, especially on more remote sections.

Q: Are the road cycling routes around Herrera de Pisuerga suitable for dog-friendly cycling?
A: While the routes themselves are generally open, the suitability for dog-friendly cycling depends on your dog's fitness, temperament, and local regulations. Many paths, especially those along the Canal de Castilla, are shared with pedestrians. If cycling with a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry water and waste bags. Always respect local signage regarding pets in natural areas.
tips: Church dating from the thirteenth century although it has been greatly reformed over time. It is located in the town of Castrillo de Riopisuerga.

Sotresgudo is a municipality and Spanish town in the province of Burgos. The municipality is part of the southern edge and less elevated sector of the Cantabrian Mountains, within the group of Las Loras.
